import { CardGrid, LinkCard, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

This is the **Build** track — for developers writing Calimero applications. If
you want the deep model of how nodes replicate, authorize, and converge state,
read the [Protocol Reference](/protocol/overview/) instead.

## What a Calimero app is

A Calimero application is a single **WebAssembly module**. You write it in Rust
against the [`calimero-sdk`](https://github.com/calimero-network/core/tree/master/crates/sdk)
and [`calimero-storage`](https://github.com/calimero-network/core/tree/master/crates/storage)
crates, compile it to `wasm32-unknown-unknown`, and install it on a node.

The module is best understood as a **shared state machine**:

- It defines a single **state** struct (`#[app::state]`) built out of CRDT
  collections — types whose concurrent edits merge deterministically.
- It exposes **methods** (`#[app::logic]`) that read and mutate that state.
- Many nodes run the *same* module over the *same* logical state. Each node
  folds the operations it has seen into a materialized copy. Because every
  collection merges order-independently, any two nodes that have seen the same
  operations compute the same state.

You never write networking, signing, or merge code. You write state + methods;
the runtime replicates the rest.

<Aside type="note" title="Mental model">
  Your app is the deterministic fold. The node is everything around it —
  identity, gossip, encryption, conflict detection. Keep your logic
  deterministic and built on CRDT collections, and convergence is automatic.
</Aside>

## Where the truth lives

Everything in this track is grounded in the code you ship against:

| Topic | Source of truth |
|-------|-----------------|
| App macros | `crates/sdk/macros/src/` |
| Environment / host calls | `crates/sdk/src/env.rs`, `crates/runtime/HOST_FUNCTIONS.md` |
| Collection types | `crates/storage/src/collections/` |
| Working examples | `apps/` (e.g. `kv-store`, `collaborative-editor`, `blobs`) |
